methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
HIMERA is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Misha Rudominsky and Oleksiy Oliynyk, having raised $2.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public frontier-tech peers in defense and adjacent sectors, including Series A companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside Seed-stage Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2M raised proxy as a baseline and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $6M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $225M (range: $60M–$500M). The median skew reflects a small sample where Series A peers significantly exceed Seed-stage valuations.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $5M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $116M. The wide dispersion reflects two distinct signals: stage-progression methodology suggests near-term valuation of ~$6M, while peer medians imply substantially higher value if HIMERA's prospects approach Series A-stage comparables. Key uncertainties include whether HIMERA's technology differentiation and market traction justify Series A multiples at Seed stage, and the limited public-market comp data (two tickers) constrains benchmarking confidence.
